expectations.text: Response has three sections: Architecture Overview, Code Implementation, Review Points
expectations.evidence: The response clearly contains '## 1. Architecture Overview', '## 2. Code Implementation', and '## 3. Review Points' as its three main sections.

expectations.text: Types/Schemas, Hooks, and Components are separated into distinct files or clearly labeled sections
expectations.evidence: Code Implementation section has clearly labeled subsections: '### Types / Schemas' (types.ts), '### Hooks' (use-multi-step-form.ts), and '### Components' (multiple component files). Each maps to a distinct file path in the directory structure.

expectations.text: No usage of 'any' type in TypeScript code
expectations.evidence: Searched all code blocks thoroughly; no instance of 'any' type found. Generic constraints like '<K extends keyof FormData>' and proper type annotations are used throughout.

expectations.text: Uses shadcn/ui components (e.g., Form, Input, Button)
expectations.evidence: Multiple shadcn/ui components are imported and used: Button (from '@/components/ui/button'), Input (from '@/components/ui/input'), Label (from '@/components/ui/label'), Card/CardContent/CardHeader/CardTitle/CardDescription (from '@/components/ui/card'), Separator (from '@/components/ui/separator').

expectations.text: Each code block includes a file path comment
expectations.evidence: Every code block starts with a file path comment: '// features/multi-step-form/types.ts', '// features/multi-step-form/hooks/use-multi-step-form.ts', '// features/multi-step-form/components/step-indicator.tsx', '// features/multi-step-form/components/form-navigation.tsx', '// features/multi-step-form/components/step-personal-info.tsx', '// features/multi-step-form/components/step-address.tsx', '// features/multi-step-form/components/step-confirmation.tsx', '// features/multi-step-form/components/multi-step-form.tsx', '// app/form/page.tsx'.

expectations.text: Uses Zod schemas for per-step validation
expectations.evidence: personalInfoSchema and addressSchema are defined separately in types.ts. The validateStep function in the hook selects the appropriate schema per step: 'const schema = step === 0 ? personalInfoSchema : addressSchema' and uses safeParse for validation. A merged formSchema is used for final submission validation.

expectations.text: State persists when navigating back to previous steps
expectations.evidence: Zustand store holds formData outside component lifecycle. The prevStep action only changes currentStep (does not reset formData). The Architecture Overview explicitly states: 'Zustand로 스텝 간 상태 공유: React의 리렌더링 트리 밖에서 폼 데이터를 관리하므로, 뒤로/앞으로 이동해도 데이터가 자연스럽게 유지된다.' Review Points reiterates: '단계를 앞뒤로 이동해도 입력값이 절대 사라지지 않는다.'

expectations.text: Uses a Compound/Headless pattern or clear step abstraction for multi-step navigation
expectations.evidence: Clear step abstraction is implemented: STEPS array defines step metadata, useMultiStepForm hook encapsulates all navigation logic (nextStep, prevStep, goToStep) with validation gating, StepIndicator and FormNavigation are reusable UI components decoupled from step content, and each step is an independent component receiving data via props. The main container (MultiStepForm) orchestrates rendering via currentStep index.
